features.summary: Disney reported Q4 2021 adjusted EPS of $0.37 versus a loss of $0.20 in the prior year, with Parks segment operating income increasing $1.6 billion year-over-year as all parks operated for the full quarter. Disney+ reached 118.1 million subscribers with 60% growth year-over-year, while ESPN+ grew to 17.1 million and Hulu to 43.8 million subscribers. Parks showed strong recovery with domestic attendance up double-digits sequentially and per capita spending up nearly 30% versus 2019 levels, driven by successful yield management strategies including the new Genie+ service adopted by one-third of Walt Disney World guests.
features.management_outlook: Management expects Disney+ subscriber growth to accelerate in the second half of fiscal 2022, driven by expanded international launches and a significant content surge from all major brands. The company plans to nearly double original content in FY22 and expand to over 160 countries by FY23. Disney+ is expected to reach peak losses in FY22 rather than FY21 due to increased content investment, but remains on track for profitability by fiscal 2024. Parks business is expected to maintain elevated per capita spending with strong forward demand, though international attendance recovery won't be substantial until late FY22.
features.risks: The company faces several headwinds including prolonged theatrical market recovery expected to impact Q1 results by $300 million, inflationary pressures on wages and content costs, and elevated operating expenses across parks and cruise operations. Linear Networks face declining subscriber trends and higher sports programming costs, with Q1 operating income expected to decrease by nearly $500 million. Supply chain disruptions are affecting certain advertising categories, and Disney+ subscriber growth may remain non-linear quarter-to-quarter as content releases vary.
features.key_drivers: Primary growth drivers include the successful implementation of yield management strategies at parks with Genie+ adoption and premium pricing, accelerating content production across all Disney+ brands with focus on local and regional programming, and expansion into sports betting opportunities through ESPN. The company is leveraging technology improvements and data analytics to enhance guest experiences and advertising targeting capabilities, while building toward a Disney metaverse that connects physical and digital experiences across all business segments.
